title: The (Few) Advantages of Retail Option Traders
text: As a retail options trader, you really have very few advantages in the market. However, compared to what you're typically probably told, it's not zero. There are a few strengths that we have. So, through this video, we're going to talk about five of them. And the goal behind this is so that unlike other areas of life where it typically makes sense to work on your weaknesses and strengthen them up, trading is all about exploiting your strengths. And part of that is understanding even what some of our strengths are. I want to start with a few areas that retail does not have an advantage. So one of these is pretty straightforward something like short-term time frames and latency dependent ideas. And it's really because if you think about things like highfrequency trading firms, they are executing in millisecond time frames. It's fully automated stuff. They literally have gone through phases where they were buying up real estate closest to the exchanges to decrease the length of their cables and then the exchanges had to come to a point where they mandated minimum cable lengths to then try to equalize it a little bit. Like that's the game that they're playing. There's absolutely no competition in that space for retail traders. And this is why when retail traders are talking about things like, "Oh, I scalp." It's like, yeah, you might intraday trade or you might trade shorter term time frames, but again, you're competing against things that

Your job isn't dependent on your ability to earn a certain sharp ratio. This is also why you see things like hedge funds and mutual funds commonly slightly underperforming the market. It's not because they don't have the skill set or the capacity to outperform the market. It's because that in order to outperform the market, the risk that they take is not worth it to slightly outperform the market as compared to if they grossly underperform, then they look terrible. So, they're actively evaluated against those kinds of performances. And this allows retail traders to actually trade things that might have an ugly path, might have big draw downs along the way.
